duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VotingDelegationLib.moveTokenDelegates uses incorrect filter `ownerOfFn(tId) == srcRep` instead of `tId != _tokenId`, wiping all delegated tokens from a delegate's checkpoint on any token transfer/mint/burn
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same ownerOf-based filter bug in moveTokenDelegates that drops delegated tokens from checkpoints. ECON-H001 selected for most complete attack economics, exploit path, and victim impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AVM-managed veNFT bribe rewards permanently locked due to ownerOf returning AutoVotingEscrow instance (not manager), failed `_owner == avm` comparison, and no-op setOriginalOwner
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same compound failure: AVE address != manager address comparison, empty setOriginalOwner, and VE._transferFrom `_to == avm` never triggering. OPS-H001 covers all three failure points most comprehensively including the VE._transferFrom angle.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RewardsDistributor.claim_many lacks AVM owner resolution logic present in claim(), sending expired AVM lock rewards to AutoVotingEscrow contract
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the missing AVM routing in claim_many vs claim(). Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the first occurrence titled 'RewardsDistributor claim_many Permanently Locks AVM Rebase Rewards', NOT the second occurrence about AVM index mapping. OPS-H005 selected for most detailed code comparison between claim and claim_many.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bribe.earned() unbounded epoch loop with WEEK=1800 causes permanent gas DoS after days of unclaimed rewards
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unbounded loop in earned() with WEEK=1800. Note: TECH2-H004 here refers to the first occurrence titled 'Unbounded Loop in Bribe earned()'. OPS-H002 selected for CRITICAL rating, detailed gas calculations, and self-reinforcing DoS analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VotingEscrow._checkpoint 255-iteration loop covers only 5.3 days with WEEK=1800, permanently corrupting global voting power after inactivity
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe 255*WEEK=459000 seconds max catch-up limitation. TECH-H013 selected for CRITICAL rating, comprehensive downstream impact analysis covering totalSupplyAtT, RewardsDistributor, and governance.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PermissionsRegistry.removeRole corrupts _addressToRoles by using global _roles array instead of per-user array in swap-and-pop
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same wrong-array swap-and-pop bug in removeRole. Note: TECH2-H007 refers to the first occurrence titled 'PermissionsRegistry removeRole Corrupts User Role Mappings', and TECH2-H008 refers to the second occurrence titled 'PermissionsRegistry.removeRole can corrupt address role arrays'. TECH-H004 selected for most precise line references and pattern description.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: delegateBySig signature replay after direct delegate() call because delegate() does not increment nonces
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same nonce management bug where delegate() bypasses nonce increment. ECON-H005 selected for complete attack economics and clear exploit scenario with specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: setSmNFTBonus changes bonus rate globally but individual smNFT checkpoints retain old values, breaking totalSupply == sum(balanceOfNFT) invariant
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same global-vs-individual checkpoint divergence after SMNFT_BONUS change. TECH-H008 selected for most detailed checkpoint mechanism analysis with specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VoterV3 voted flag remains true when all gauges are killed because _vote only calls voting() when _usedWeight > 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same state issue where voted flag persists after poke/vote with all dead gauges. Note: TECH2-H008 refers to the first occurrence titled 'VoterV3 _vote Leaves Token Permanently Locked'. TECH-H009 selected for most precise code path analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AutoVotingEscrow.lockOwner returns incorrect owner for non-existent/released tokenIds due to default mapping value of 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same default-mapping-index bug in lockOwner. TECH-H010 selected for more complete analysis including releaseLock interaction.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Merging normal lock into SMNFT burns tokens via _black.burn() without decrementing supply variable, causing VotingEscrow insolvency
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same supply tracking bug when merging into SMNFT. Note: TECH2-H005 refers to the first occurrence titled 'Merging into SMNFT Burns Tokens Without Decrementing Supply'. OPS-H003 selected for most comprehensive analysis including updateToSMNFT path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VotingEscrow.merge does not check source lock is active, allowing expired locks to merge into SMNFT for free bonus voting power
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the missing source-lock-expiry check in merge. ECON-H006 selected for attack economics and clear impact quantification.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RewardsDistributor._checkpoint_token 20-iteration loop covers only 10 hours with WEEK=1800, permanently losing undistributed rebase tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the 20-iteration cap creating 10-hour maximum catch-up. OPS-H007 selected for CRITICAL rating and most detailed analysis of the token loss mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bribe.withdraw silently skips when amount > balanceOf[tokenId], creating phantom voting weight in bribe contracts
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the silent-skip withdrawal bug. ECON2-H008 selected for more concrete attack scenario involving bribe contract replacement.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bribe.notifyRewardAmount records full reward amount without balance-delta check, creating insolvency with fee-on-transfer tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing balance-delta verification in notifyRewardAmount. ECON-H023 selected for highest severity rating and most complete analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VoterV3.poke() lacks epochVoteEnd restriction present in vote(), allowing last-hour vote weight manipulation via deposit_for/increase_amount triggering poke
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All exploit the same missing epochVoteEnd check in poke. OPS-H006 selected for most comprehensive technical analysis covering deposit_for authentication bypass, specific code references contrasting poke vs vote checks.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SMNFT split does not adjust smNFTBalance, creating accounting divergence when combined with SMNFT_BONUS changes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe smNFTBalance not being updated during split and its interaction with bonus rate changes. ECON-H011 selected for most detailed accounting analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AutoVotingEscrowManager.getTotalVotingPower unbounded nested loop over all AVMs and locks causes gas DoS
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same nested unbounded loop DoS. Note: TECH2-H009 refers to the first occurrence titled 'Unbounded Loop in AutoVotingEscrowManager getTotalVotingPower'. Selected for clearest technical analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: create_lock allows any user to set isSMNFT=true without access control, enabling unrestricted permanent voting power creation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the missing access control on isSMNFT parameter. Note: TECH2-H006 refers to the first occurrence titled 'Unrestricted SMNFT Creation'. Selected for more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TokenHandler._blacklist returns early before emitting Blacklisted event, breaking event-based monitoring
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the unreachable event emission due to early return in _blacklist. Note: TECH2-H009 here refers to the second occurrence titled 'TokenHandler blacklist emits no Blacklisted event'. Selected for more detailed analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: VoterV3._vote division-by-zero when _totalVoteWeight=0 because all selected gauges are dead, potentially due to inconsistent liveness checks between two loops
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the division-by-zero risk when all gauges are dead. Note: TECH2-H007 refers to the second occurrence titled 'VoterV3._vote division-by-zero'. OPS-H017 selected for most detailed analysis of the inconsistent liveness predicate scenario.

deduplication_analysis._note_on_duplicate_ids: The input contains duplicate hypothesis_id values in the TECH2 series (H003-H009 appear twice with different content from two separate specialist runs). In the threats_to_remove list, TECH2-H003/H004/H005 each have one occurrence that should be removed (the first: claim_many/bribe-DoS/SMNFT-supply respectively) and one unique occurrence that should be retained (AVM-index-mapping/AVM-steal/reentrancy respectively). TECH2-H007 and TECH2-H008 have both occurrences removed. TECH2-H006 and TECH2-H009 have both occurrences retained as representatives of different groups. The unique_threats_retained list includes the second occurrences of TECH2-H003 (AVM index mapping), TECH2-H004 (AVM steal via approval), TECH2-H005 (safeTransferFrom reentrancy), and TECH2-H006 (bribe token spam) alongside the first occurrences of TECH2-H006 (unrestricted SMNFT) and TECH2-H009 (AVM getTotalVotingPower DoS) and second TECH2-H009 (blacklist event bug).
